blurb: A silvery-green rosette of incurved leaves lined with soft white bristles and faint leaf-windows. Another easy-going, shade-tolerant Haworthia for a bright shelf.

care.light: Bright, indirect light. It tolerates lower light better than most succulents but colours and stays compact best with strong filtered light; harsh sun scorches it.
care.water: Soak-and-dry — a deep drink, then fully dry before the next, easing off in winter.
care.dormancy: Grows in the mild seasons and rests in extreme heat and cold.
care.feeding: Very light feeding once or twice in the growing season.
care.potting: Repot every 2–3 years, dividing offsets as it clumps.
care.bloom: Small white tubular flowers on a slender stalk.
